title: Unoccupied Space
excerpt: An unoccupied space is a space the current game state treats as open for valid placement, arrival, or movement because nothing else is presently resolved as occupying it.
markdown:
# Unoccupied Space

An unoccupied space is a space the current game state treats as open because nothing else is presently resolved as occupying it. Rules often require an unoccupied space when a creature, object, or effect needs a valid place to appear, move, or be placed.

## Space State

- A space is unoccupied when no creature, object, or other present effect is currently using that space as part of its resolved position
- A space being unoccupied does not automatically mean every rule can use it; other requirements such as range, visibility, support, or terrain can still matter
- Unoccupied space is mainly a placement and movement state, not a guarantee that the space is otherwise ideal or safe

## Rules Use

### Arrival And Placement
- Teleportation, summoning, conjuration, and other placement rules often require an unoccupied destination space
- A created object or effect can fail to place if the intended space is already taken
- A rule that asks for an unoccupied space is checking availability before the new presence is resolved there

### Movement Resolution
- Many movement rules assume a creature can end movement only in an unoccupied space unless another rule explicitly overrides that restriction
- Forced movement and repositioning effects may also require the destination space to remain open at the moment of resolution
- A space can stop being unoccupied if another creature or effect reaches it first

### Separate From Other Restrictions
- An unoccupied space can still be difficult to use because of hazards, [[area_of_effect|areas of effect]], cover geometry, or other battlefield conditions
- The space is merely open in the sense that it is not already filled by another resolved presence

## Common Uses

- Choosing a valid teleport destination
- Summoning a creature into play
- Creating an object or effect at a location
- Ending movement in a legal space after repositioning or travel
